diet_advice.current_diet_assessment: Daily consumption of soft drinks and junk foods is the primary concern, contributing to high BMI and low HDL.
priority_changes: Strictly eliminate all soft drinks and junk foods.
Increase daily intake of fruits and vegetables to 2-3 servings each.
Focus on portion control for all meals to achieve gradual weight loss.
Include a handful of nuts/seeds daily to improve HDL cholesterol.
diet_advice.breakfast: Oats Upma with mixed vegetables (1 small bowl) — Provides complex carbohydrates and fiber, aiding satiety and blood sugar control.
diet_advice.lunch: 1 small bowl Brown Rice, 1 small bowl Dal, 1 medium Katori of mixed vegetable subzi, 1 small bowl Curd — Offers balanced macros with good fiber and protein for satiety and blood sugar.
diet_advice.dinner: 2 Whole Wheat Roti with 1 medium Katori of lentil curry and a side salad — Lighter, fiber-rich meal that supports digestion and portion control before bed.
foods_to_avoid: Soft drinks — High in sugar, contributes to obesity and poor blood sugar control.
Processed junk foods (biscuits, chips, fried snacks) — High in unhealthy fats, sodium, and refined carbs, detrimental to heart health and weight.
Deep-fried foods (e.g., Puri, Pakora) — Adds unnecessary calories and unhealthy fats, impacting weight and heart health.
diet_advice.clinical_note: Review necessity of statin therapy for patient with history of heart attack and low HDL, especially being off OADs.
